notes: Forward returns at 5/20/60d horizons overlap across consecutive daily signals; effective independent sample is far below nominal n. Directional evidence only — not grounds for mechanical parameter changes.
v8 telemetry (divergence, hostile_review, regime driver/basis) began accruing mid-June 2026; sections 2-4 deepen with time.
Temporal z baseline excludes the most recent observation; generate-signals consumes the stored baseline next morning (one-day staleness, immaterial at window 60).

tactical.falsifier: A daily close back above the SMA50 ($301.93) with RSI reclaiming 50 and the cohort gap narrowing would confirm reversal and push the score more negative; conversely a break below the 52w low ($257.35) on margin-related news flips me toward neutral/positive.

positional.falsifier: A book-to-bill print >1.10 with backlog YoY re-accelerating, or a major program award (Golden Dome/NGAD), plus EPS revisions turning positive would validate the deep-buy — pushing toward -75; conversely a B/B <0.95 with negative backlog YoY or a guide-down flips me positive.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that forward P/E is actually running a discount to cohort (>10%) alongside op margin recovering back toward 13-14% would flip strategic to a buy tilt; conversely a DoD budget shift to CR-uncertainty/sequester risk deepens the trim.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp >5% single-day drop on non-fundamental news (e.g. macro/China headline) would flip this to a tactical BUY; a break of the 50-day ($1703) on heavy volume would flip it to trim.

positional.falsifier: A decisive close below the 200-day SMA ($1349) — a ~24% drawdown — would flip this positive (trim); a >15% drawdown from highs would flip it negative (buy the dip).

strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E confirmed above 45x combined with book-to-bill falling below 1.0 or TSMC+Samsung+Intel all guiding capex cuts would push this clearly positive (trim); conversely a >15% drawdown or forward P/E below 25x flips it to a strategic BUY.

tactical.falsifier: QUAL factor flow flipping negative vs SPY (rotation out of quality into cyclicals/value) with LIN breaking below the 50-day ($513.51) would flip this to a trim.

positional.falsifier: Break below the 200-day ($471.67) or LIN/APD spread blowing out negative (LIN materially lagging its closest peer) would flip the positional sign to trim.

strategic.falsifier: A confirmed structural margin/ROCE decline (management guiding lower, price/mix-ex-FX turning negative) OR a mega-scale H2 project announcement would flip this — the former deepens the trim, the latter flips it to buy.

tactical.falsifier: Price breaking decisively below the $349 52w low on heavy volume, or RSI failing to reclaim 50 while the death-cross gap widens, would flip this to a momentum-negative (positive score) setup.

positional.falsifier: A confirmed Azure constant-currency deceleration below 22% at next earnings, or MSFT making new lows below $349 with margins compressing, would validate the bear derating and flip the sign positive.

strategic.falsifier: Azure CC growth confirmed decelerating below 22% for two consecutive quarters, or an OpenAI partnership unwind/adverse AGI-clause trigger, would break the secular thesis and flip the sign positive.

tactical.falsifier: RSI spiking >70 with a parabolic move would flip to a genuine trim; RSI <35 on a rate-driven flush would flip to buy.

positional.falsifier: 10Y falling toward ~4.0% (widening ENB's spread back above 100bps) or a dividend-growth guidance raise would flip this to a buy; conversely a 10Y spike above 5% making the spread negative would justify a stronger trim.

strategic.falsifier: A dividend cut or coverage deterioration, or structural Mainline volume loss to Trans Mountain, would flip strongly positive; BoC/Fed cutting cycle beginning with ENB yield spread re-widening past 150bps would flip negative (buy).

tactical.falsifier: Two consecutive closes back above the 50-day SMA ($14.94) with LME copper making fresh multi-week highs would flip this to a buy-side tactical.

positional.falsifier: GLNCY dropping below the 200-day SMA ($12.70) alongside COPX rolling over and HY OAS widening past ~3.5% would flip positioning to trim/sell.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained LME copper break below ~$3.80/lb with rising visible inventories and evidence of a deepening (not recovering) earnings trough would flip strategic to trim.

tactical.falsifier: A sustained close above the 50DMA ($39.04) on rising volume would flip this to a tactical buy; a break below $32.84 would confirm downside.

positional.falsifier: Confirmation of accelerating IBIT net outflows alongside falling price (distribution) would flip me to neutral/positive; conversely a decisive 200DMA reclaim would push me deeper negative.

strategic.falsifier: Evidence the 4-year cycle is decisively broken (sustained new highs well past historical top windows on ETF flows) flips this negative; a macro liquidity shock or ETF exodus flips it clearly positive.

tactical.falsifier: MXN/USD breaking decisively below 17.0 (peso strength) with RSI turning up through 50 would flip this to a buy tilt; a break above 18.0 would flip it to a trim tilt.

positional.falsifier: A break of the 200-day ($98.79) on weak volume trends or MXN above 18.5 would flip this positive (trim); reclaiming the 50-day with a peso rally would deepen the buy.

strategic.falsifier: A durable MXN regime break above ~19 (peso depreciation) or a structural downgrade to Mexican consumer volumes would flip this to a trim; confirmation of stable peso + accelerating dividend growth deepens the buy.

tactical.falsifier: A close decisively below the $15.89 SMA200 on rising volume (flips to +) or a >5% breakout above $17 on an oil/dividend catalyst (flips to -).

positional.falsifier: A government announcement redirecting cash flow away from dividends toward mandated investment, or BRL breaking above 5.30 on fiscal deterioration (flips to +); conversely a confirmed dividend hike with oil firming would push further negative.

strategic.falsifier: A structural policy shift — either a durable dividend-policy guarantee / privatization signal (flips negative) or a formal move to mandate lower fuel prices and higher capex at the expense of shareholders (pushes more positive).

tactical.falsifier: Drewry WCI composite breaking sharply higher (>20% in 2-3 weeks) on a supply shock would flip this to a buy; a decisive RSI break below 45 with a close under the 50d would confirm the trim.

strategic.falsifier: A decisive structural down-cycle signal — ocean EBIT turning negative for consecutive quarters with management guiding to sustained losses, or P/B pushing back above ~1.0 on a rate spike removing the value cushion — would flip the sign.

positional.falsifier: XBI 90d rolling negative or a biotech funding freeze (IPO/M&A drought) would remove the bookings tailwind and flip positional to neutral/trim.

strategic.falsifier: A confirmed simultaneous China capex collapse plus material NIH budget cuts, or bioprocessing commentary from DHR/Sartorius/Repligen turning back toward destocking, would flip strategic to neutral/trim.

positional.falsifier: cRPO decelerating to <18% on the next print, or negative 60/90d EPS revisions accelerating, would confirm structural weakness and flip the sign positive.

strategic.falsifier: cRPO holding 20%+ with Now Assist Pro Plus ASP inflection and forward PE confirmed near/below 50x would flip strategic negative (buy); conversely a Fed re-tightening pushing real rate sustainably >2% would push it more positive.

tactical.falsifier: A break back below the 200-SMA ($529) on expanding volume, or a spike in VIX above ~22 signaling fear regime onset, would flip this toward a buy setup (negative) as drawdown deepens past 12%.

positional.falsifier: A Q2 cross-border volume print below ~8% FX-neutral, or negative EPS revisions turning the 90d trend down, would remove the fundamental support and flip this to neutral/trim (positive).

strategic.falsifier: Passage of interchange/network-fee legislation to a floor vote or enactment, OR measurable ('material') stablecoin displacement at real-economy merchants, would flip this to trim (positive) as a genuine thesis break.

tactical.falsifier: Q2 print shows procedure growth <12% paired with explicit US procedure-share loss to Hugo, or transition quantified as material — that flips this from a buy setup to a trim.
